business_story: As a CAA SORA assessor I want swarm-aware SORA evidence and guidance so that I can set SAIL and OSO requirements defensibly for multi-agent operations.
reasoning: REQ002 describes a large capability ('swarm-aware SORA evidence') that subsumes multiple sub-features: swarm SAIL determination, swarm OSO determination, and swarm-specific risk quantification. The requirement conflates evidence review with assessment decision-making. Granularity is inconsistent with other REQs (some are specific tasks, this is an entire assessment domain).
recommendation: Decompose into: SORA SAIL Determination (swarm-specific), SORA OSO Determination (swarm-specific), Swarm Risk Quantification Evidence Review. Clarify whether the system generates SORA guidance or merely compiles evidence for human assessors.

feature_name: Insurance Artefact Upload & Review + Ground Survey Upload & Parse
business_story: As a CAA authorisation officer I want insurance and a ground survey so that third-party risk is both transferable and factually grounded.
reasoning: REQ013 combines two logically independent features: (1) insurance policy review (legal/financial domain) and (2) ground survey ingestion and geospatial analysis (physical/environmental domain). They have different data formats, validation rules, and review workflows. Single-feature treatment obscures distinct capabilities.
recommendation: Split REQ013 into REQ013a (Insurance Artefact Upload & Review) and REQ013b (Ground Survey Upload & Geospatial Parse). Define separate validation rules, evidence requirements, and reviewer roles for each.

finding_title: Four Critical Features Have No User Story (REQ017-020)
feature_name: Public & Stakeholder Engagement, Security Classification Handling, Environmental Impact Assessment, Post-Trial Close-out
business_story: As a CAA officer I want [stakeholder engagement | classified evidence handling | environmental assessment | post-trial archival] so that [CAP 1616 compliance | security compliance | environmental compliance | institutional learning].
reasoning: REQ017-020 explicitly state 'No user story covers...' and instead describe what is 'strongly implied' or 'absent'. These are critical regulatory requirements (CAP 1616, CAP 722H/JSP 440, environmental regulations, institutional audit trails) but have no stakeholder perspective or acceptance criteria. This creates ambiguity about system behaviour, scope, and success metrics.
recommendation: Immediately author user stories for REQ017-020 by consulting: (1) CAP 1616 consultation requirements, (2) CAP 722H/JSP 440 classification handling rules, (3) environmental impact assessment standards, (4) post-trial audit and closeout procedures. Engage stakeholders (community liaisons, security officers, environmental assessors, programme managers) to define acceptance criteria.

finding_title: Operator-Private Feedback vs Regulator Decision Record Not Addressed
feature_name: Operator-Private Feedback vs Regulator Decision Record Segregation
business_story: As an applicant I want my feedback and revision history to remain private so that regulator decision records are separated from internal deliberations.
reasoning: The context describes two user journeys: (1) operator-facing pre-submission preparation and (2) regulator-facing decision mode. No REQ explicitly addresses how these modes remain segregated. If feedback from mode (1) leaks into mode (2), the system violates applicant privacy and may compromise regulator independence. This is a critical architectural feature, not a functional nicety.
recommendation: Define F25 (Operator-Private Feedback vs Regulator Decision Record Segregation) explicitly. Clarify: (1) which artefacts/annotations are operator-private, (2) which are visible to regulators, (3) how the system prevents accidental cross-contamination, (4) what audit trail exists for segregation enforcement. This may require dual-channel record management or role-based view filtering.

finding_title: No CRUD Operations for Revision, Withdrawal, or Re-opening
business_story: As a CAA programme manager I want to withdraw, re-open, or revise trial authorizations so that the system supports reversible phased gates.
reasoning: REQ016 promises 'reversible' phased gates, but no REQ specifies how authorization withdrawal, case re-opening, or revision rollback is achieved. Only 'approval forward' is implicit. Missing: (1) Withdraw authorization (reverse F18 approval), (2) Re-open closed trial, (3) Rollback to previous version. These are essential CRUD operations for reversibility.
recommendation: Define three missing features: (1) Authorization Withdrawal, (2) Trial Re-opening, (3) Case Revision Rollback. Clarify: (1) what triggers these actions, (2) who can initiate them, (3) what audit trail is created, (4) what happens to associated artefacts.

business_story: As a CAA case officer I want regulatory clauses to be version-pinned to the trial authorization so that clause interpretation disputes are avoided.
reasoning: No REQ mentions clause versioning. However, the system must manage regulatory compliance checks across multiple trial authorizations over multi-year periods. If clause interpretations evolve (e.g., CAP 722H guidance updates), older authorizations may become uninterpretable without version records. This is a hidden, non-functional feature critical for long-term regulatory defensibility.
recommendation: Define F23 (Clause-Library Version Pinning) explicitly. Clarify: (1) which clause sources are versioned (CAP 722H, CAP 1616, etc.), (2) how versions are pinned to cases at authorization time, (3) how version changes are tracked, (4) how auditors can reproduce historical compliance determinations.

finding_title: Classified Evidence Access Control Not Detailed
business_story: As a CAA security officer I want evidence to be automatically tagged and segregated by classification level so that access control and disclosure are enforced.
reasoning: REQ018 notes that 'classified MOD evidence is presented to the CAA under CAP 722H / JSP 440 without compromise'. This requires: (1) classification tagging on ingest, (2) role-based view filtering, (3) redaction rules, (4) compartmentalization enforcement. No REQ specifies these mechanisms, yet they are non-negotiable for MOD involvement.
recommendation: Define F24 (Classification-Aware Evidence Segregation) as a hidden sub-feature. Clarify: (1) classification tags (UK caveat, MOD-only, releasable, etc.), (2) role-to-clearance mapping, (3) automatic redaction rules, (4) audit trail for access. Work with MOD security and CAA to define compartmentalization rules.

finding_title: Swarm SORA and Swarm Decision-Making Features Overlap
feature_name: SORA 2.0 Assessment (F03) vs Swarm Decision-Making Architecture Review (F09)
business_story: Both features require evidence of swarm collective behaviour; F03 (SORA) focuses on risk quantification, F09 (architecture) focuses on decision-making logic.
reasoning: REQ002 (F03 SORA) and REQ008 (F09 architecture) both require 'swarm evidence'. SORA needs swarm risk models and emergent-behaviour test data. Architecture review needs decision-making logic and emergent-behaviour evidence. The input artefacts likely overlap (same test data, same behaviour models). Unclear whether F03 and F09 are independent workflows or sequential.
recommendation: Clarify dependency and artifact sharing: (1) Does F03 consume outputs from F09, or are they independent? (2) Which features own the 'emergent behaviour evidence'? (3) Are swarm risk models generated by F09 and consumed by F03, or are they separate? Define clear boundaries to avoid duplicate work.

finding_title: Granularity Mismatch: Epic vs Feature vs Sub-feature
business_story: Some REQs describe broad epics (e.g., F03 SORA, F09 swarm decision), others describe specific tasks (e.g., F14 insurance upload, F16 crew competency).
reasoning: REQ002 (SORA) spans multiple assessment sub-tasks and integrates evidence from multiple sources. REQ014 (crew training) is a single, focused review task. REQ013 (insurance + ground survey) combines two unrelated tasks. This inconsistency makes it difficult to estimate scope, plan sprints, or define test strategy. Some REQs are epics, others are features, others are sub-features.
recommendation: Apply consistent granularity criteria: (1) Define feature size (e.g., 'can be completed in 1-2 sprints by one team'), (2) Re-scope large REQs (REQ002, REQ008, REQ009) as epics with explicitly named features, (3) Group small REQs (REQ013, REQ014) where appropriate. Use a feature-size rubric (story points, person-days) to normalize.

business_story: As a CAA environmental assessor I want documented environmental impact evidence so that noise, species disturbance, and battery-fire risk are assessed.
reasoning: REQ019 notes that 'no user story covers noise, species disturbance at dusk/night, or battery-fire environmental risk on ground'. These are distinct regulatory concerns (noise pollution, wildlife protection, fire safety) with different evidence types, assessment methodologies, and stakeholders. REQ019 lumps all three together without acceptance criteria or role clarity.
recommendation: Decompose REQ019 into three features: (1) Noise Impact Assessment (dB levels, mitigation), (2) Wildlife Disturbance Assessment (species, timing, mitigation), (3) Battery Fire Risk Assessment (ground fire hazard, containment). Define stakeholder roles (environmental officer, wildlife assessor, fire safety officer) and acceptance criteria for each.

finding_title: Panel Decision Override Logging Not Explicit
business_story: As a CAA panel member I want to log overrides and decisions so that all decision points are audited and reversals are traceable.
reasoning: REQ016 describes 'reversible' gates but does not specify how reversals are logged or what happens when a panel reverses a previous decision. This is critical for audit trails and regulatory defensibility. Who logs the reversal? What rationale is captured? How is the audit trail protected from tampering?
recommendation: Define F26 (Panel Override & Decision Logging). Clarify: (1) what system actions trigger override logging, (2) what data is captured (actor, timestamp, rationale, prior state), (3) who can initiate overrides, (4) what audit trail is immutable, (5) how overrides are reflected in case history.
